title: Oldenlandia peduncularis
taxonomy: Oldenlandia peduncularis belongs to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family comprises approximately 600 genera and 13,000 species of flowering plants, many of which are known for their medicinal and ornamental value. Oldenlandia peduncularis is a genus of flowering plants that is native to tropical Africa, parts of Asia, and the Pacific Islands. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants, with many species exhibiting unique characteristics and adaptations. Oldenlandia peduncularis is a member of the Rubiaceae family, which is widely distributed across the tropics and subtropics.
uses: Oldenlandia peduncularis has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help in treating various health issues. In some parts of the world, it is used to treat fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The plant is also believed to have antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and skin infections. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Oldenlandia peduncularis for medicinal use.

intro: Oldenlandia peduncularis, also known as Lian Qiao or Oldenlandia, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia peduncularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Oldenlandia is used to make herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into various Chinese medicinal formulas, such as Lian Qiao San, to treat various health conditions.
